Hello @Christian_Cohrs1 

First of all I'd like to commend the creation of this blog demonstrating the capabilities of cloud lead SSO to SAP GUI for Windows.

I have a question around the actual licencing of the SAP Secure Login Service.

I assume it is best practice to have a "Non-Production" instance aligned to SAP SCI IAS Test and "Production" instance aligned to SAP SCI IAS Production.

With this is mind:

  • Does the SAP SLS license for blocks of users include the ability to create more than one SLS instance as part of the license
  • or is there a separate license needed per SLS instance?
  • It is my understanding that SAP Secure Login Service uses a metered logon model, not per‑instance licensing so licensing of service actually permits two instances - one per IAS 

Please can you clarify. If my assumptions above are correct, I assume it is necessary to specify the quota needed at global account level when paying for service subscription.

Thanks in advance

Mike

entry.title: Role-based process overview: get the right content tailored to your needs!
entry.content:
As organizations push for smarter, more adaptive ways of working, the need for tools that support personalization and efficiency has never been clearer. That's why SAP Signavio introduced the Role-based process overview, giving individuals a clearer, more tailored view of the processes that matter most. 

Whether you're a domain expert who needs quick access to information that matters or a new joiner eager to get up to speed, this capability makes sure everyone gets the personalized content they need to work more efficiently. This leads to faster onboarding, stronger process adoption, and more time spent on high-value work. With Role-based process overview, SAP Signavio helps your teams stay focused, aligned, and empowered to make confident decisions by putting the right process information at their fingertips.

To enable Role-based process overview, an administrator needs to activate 'General - My Process Overview' in the feature sets.

Personalized process content at your fingertips

The Role-based process overview within SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub provides personalized content tailored to each user's unique role and access rights in the dictionary, streamlining how they access and interact with essential information. 

Its intuitive design facilitates easy navigation between elements, ensuring you maintain a clear, up-to-date view of process-related information. By centralizing essential resources, the Role-based process overview enhances your ability to streamline workflows, manage tasks efficiently, and ensure no critical information is overlooked.

How it works

You can now effortlessly access the right process content tailored to your role without the hassle of complex navigation. Upon accessing SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub, you can:

  1. Find "My Process Overview" When you login locate the My Process Overview panel on the home page
  2. Complete the Quick Setup On your first visit, a guided setup wizard will walk you through the process.
  3. Select Your Role(s) Choose the role(s) that match your responsibilities from your organization's dictionary entries. This ensures you see exactly what's relevant to your job.
  4. Confirm and Save Once you've selected your role(s), confirm your choices to activate your overview.
  5. Enjoy Your Personalized View That's it! My Process Overview generates automatically and shows you everything you need in one place: 
    1. Processes linked to your role(s)
    2. Activities assigned to your role(s)
    3. RACI assignments for each activity (who is Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed)

Work Smarter Return anytime to quickly access role-relevant content without digging through navigation-reducing search time and supporting faster onboarding and better process adoption.

 

Benefits of the role-based process overview capability

Personalized experience

  • Enhanced relevance: Users see what is contextually important to their roles, ensuring that critical information is never missed.
  • Single view of information: All essential details are accessible on a single page, reducing the likelihood of missing important updates or insights.
  • Access-aware recommendations: Users only see content they’re permitted to access.

Time savings

  • No manual navigation: The overview intelligently displays only the most relevant information, allowing users to quickly access the information they need.
  • Efficient workflows: Manual filtering is eliminated, enabling users to focus on strategic tasks.

Minimized administrative tasks

  • Less manual configuration: Reduced need for setting up role-specific views and paths, simplifying the administrative workload.

Transforming tomorrow

This is just the beginning. Experience the future of business process management with SAP Signavio. Dive into the personalized world of process modeling and elevate your transformation journey.

Start the EnableNow step-by-step tutorial for this capability for end users

entry.title: Re: Validation in Profitability Segment
entry.content:
Hi @andrea_peroni ,

Please refer to customizing activity documentation Assign Characteristic Groups for Assignment Screen (Tcode KE4G):

Assign Characteristic Groups for Assignment Screen

In this step, you structure the "Assignments to Profitability Segments" dialog box (the assignment screen) for business transactions, such as:

  • Direct postings from Financial Accounting
  • Maintenance of settlement rules for CO orders or projects
  • Internal cost allocations

To organize the layout of the assignment screen, you assign a characteristic group to each business transaction.

Furthermore, you can construct entry aids for the business transactions. In the entry aids, you can save combinations of characteristic values that are frequently required during assignment. These entry aids can then be made available to users making assignments to profitability segments. For more information, see the documentation for Profitability Analysis.

Prerequisite

You need to have created a characteristic group (Tcode KEPA)

Activities

Assign the Characteristic groups to the desired business transactions and create any relevant entry aids. You can also create entry aids for a business transaction without assigning a characteristic group.

If you delete a characteristic group assignment, any associated entry aids will also be deleted.

Note

If a transaction does not have a characteristic group assigned to it, users can enter values for all of the characteristics in the operating concern.

If this assignment of characteristic groups to business transactions is too restrictive, you can use the standard enhancement "COPA0003" to define a more flexible way of assigning characteristic groups within individual transactions.

If your need is depending on a specific GL account, you should use enhancement (Tcode CMOD) COPA0003.

Please let me know if you have doubt.

I hope it helps.

BR,

Filipe

But at no point was it ever mentioned to us that it was going to be a forced move. Only found that out this week when we got the SAP email (glad we at least got that!). Our current Indeed XML feed that is being discontinued is custom-filtered,  and initial conversations with Indeed this week are suggesting that their alternative API solution can't offer custom API query filters to replicate what we currently have set up. Meeting them tomorrow to see where we can go from here. Not a good situation at all!

entry.title: Event Recap — SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days
entry.content:
🌟 SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026

Event Recap — How SAP Is Enabling Connected, Customer‑Driven Product Innovation

For three days, experts from engineering, R&D, manufacturing, quality, compliance, IT, and project management came together to explore one of the most pressing questions facing modern product organizations:

How do we build products faster, smarter, and more responsibly in an increasingly complex world?

Each day examined this question through a different lens — discrete industries, process industries, and project & portfolio management — yet a shared storyline ran across all discussions. Attendees repeatedly highlighted challenges around fragmented product data, heavy compliance workloads, disconnected processes, and rising expectations for AI-enabled efficiency.

Across the event, one thing became clear: Integrated, intelligent PLM is no longer optional. It’s becoming the operational backbone of modern product organizations.

🔍 Three Cross‑Event Themes That Defined the Week

1. Product data fragmentation remains the biggest barrier

Whether teams manage CAD files, formulation data, specifications, or project plans, many participants described workflows that still depend on disconnected tools, inconsistent handovers, and manual reconciliation. These issues slow development and create risks long before a product reaches the shop floor.

2. Handover friction is still one of the costliest issues

Across industries, attendees shared how difficult it remains to move product definitions cleanly from engineering → manufacturing, formulation → production, and planning → execution. Every time a document, recipe, product structure or bill of materials changes hands, teams lose time, context, or accuracy.

This was one of the clearest signals from the entire event: handovers matter as much as the work itself.

3. AI is shifting from potential to practice

Perhaps the biggest mindset shift was the acceptance of AI as a practical, embedded partner in everyday workflows. Across the three days, participants responded strongly to examples of AI:

  • identifying risks earlier
  • supporting change impact analysis
  • assisting formulation decisions
  • forecasting project schedules
  • enhancing decision-making with natural-language insights

The enthusiasm showed that organizations are ready for AI that is useful, contextual, and integrated directly into their PLM workflows.

🛠️ What Resonated Across the Three Days

Even though each day focused on a different domain, attendees gravitated toward similar ideas — all centered on simplifying complexity and strengthening the flow of product data.

🔧 Day 1 — Discrete Industries

Conversations centered on the importance of engineering data that flows: CAD, product structures, visualization, governed change management, and supply-chain-ready definitions. Engagement grew whenever the link between engineering and manufacturing became seamless and traceable.

🧪 Day 2 — Process Industries

Participants in process industries highlighted the complexity of connecting formulation, specification management, labeling, and compliance. Reactions showed strong interest in structured property management, automated labeling, collaboration scenarios, and native integration with downstream processes.

📊 Day 3 — Portfolio & Project Management

Project and portfolio leaders focused on predictability: aligning resources, forecasting risks, synchronizing plans with execution, and managing ETO complexity. Interest peaked whenever real-time data and AI provided clarity in situations that are normally opaque.

🌐 Bringing It All Together — A Future Defined by Flexibility

Across all three days of the SAP Solution for PLM Info Days 2026, one insight rose above everything else: there is no single PLM journey. Every organization attending navigates a different mix of tools, processes, partners, and regulatory requirements — and SAP’s strategy recognizes and embraces that diversity.

Discrete manufacturers wrestle with CAD-heavy engineering environments; process industry teams manage formulations, specifications, and compliance; and project-driven organizations balance resources, risks, and constantly shifting priorities. Yet despite these differences, a consistent truth connected every conversation:

Product data only delivers value when it is connected — and each customer needs the freedom to define how that connection happens.

That is why SAP’s solutions for PLM approach centers on choice. Rather than prescribing a rigid architecture, SAP empowers customers through three complementary paths:

  • Direct CAD Integration
     For engineering-led companies that want design files, product structures, and change processes flowing cleanly into the SAP Business Suite.
  • Cloud-Native Product Data Management
     For teams looking for collaborative capabilities on SAP BTP — formulation, specification governance, product structures — fully integrated with SAP’s operational backbone.
  • PLM System Integration
     For organizations invested in external PLM, enabling bi-directional synchronization and smooth orchestration of product data across hybrid landscapes.

What ties these paths together is the SAP Business Suite — the system of record that anchors product truth, unifies supply chain and execution processes, and unlocks operational value from engineering and R&D decisions.

The strongest takeaway from the event was not about any single tool or capability. It was about empowerment. Customers want to modernize on their own terms, evolve their PLM landscape without disruption, and integrate what works today with what they want tomorrow. SAP’s portfolio — from SAP Engineering Control Center and SAP Integrated Product Development on SAP Business Technology Platform to SAP Project & Resource Management, SAP Enterprise Portfolio & Project Management, and PLM System Integration — gives them exactly that freedom.

✨ Final Thoughts — How This Blog Series Fits Together

This overall recap, along with the three deep-dive blogs for discrete industries, process industries, and project & portfolio management, forms a complete picture of SAP’s flexibility-first PLM strategy.

Each blog explores a different entry point into the product lifecycle, but together they show how SAP enables companies to build their own path toward a connected, intelligent, lifecycle-driven product development ecosystem.

Rather than forcing customers into a single definition of PLM, SAP provides a flexible foundation — grounded in the SAP Business Suite, enhanced by cloud innovation, and extended through open integration — so every organization can design a PLM journey that fits their industry, their maturity, and their future.

Continue Your Journey with SAP solutions for PLM

If you missed a session or want to revisit highlights, you can still watch the SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 on‑demand.

We’ll continue deep‑diving into engineering, formulation, compliance, project management, and AI‑enabled product development throughout the year. Make sure to bookmark our upcoming webinars to stay ahead of the latest innovations.

To stay connected with the PLM community of SAP:

  • Follow us on LinkedIn for insights, updates, and customer stories
  • Join the SAP Community and follow the Product Lifecycle Management tag
  • Explore the latest product tour on sap.com/plm to see how our solutions help streamline product data and accelerate innovation

Thank you for joining us at SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 — we’re excited to continue shaping the future of connected intelligent product development with you.

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content:
Hello Stefan

Thanks for your continuous valuable advices.

In my case we have got two SAP notes released to fix the issue in most of countries that for posting from TPP where more than 999 lines could be generated.

However we still have another issue that few countries especially large legal entities in EU have many foreign VAT Reg in different EU countries and require alternative GL account when post tax payable for each VAT Reg. just like standard program RFUMSV00. But TPP App doesn`t have this feature right now and my ticket has been put as "pending release". 

So the only option for such countries then would be to use RFUMSV00 which will create batch input for posting, like FB41. and the question come back then, how do we summarize in this case so that we won`t exceed 999 lines? 

You mentioned FB01 as such transactions will not be able to be summarized, do you see any possibility for posting via RFUMSV00 batch input? 

Appreciate again your advices.

BR/Jack

entry.title: Re: High importance flag in outlook from ABAP for email u...
entry.content:
Here is a sample using the BCS classes (which should be the preference now anyways).

DATA: req       TYPE REF TO cl_bcs,
      doc       TYPE REF TO cl_document_bcs,
      sender    TYPE REF TO if_sender_bcs,
      recipient TYPE REF TO if_recipient_bcs,
      sent      TYPE os_boolean.

TRY.
*   Generate send request along with document for sending
    req = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
    doc = cl_document_bcs=>create_document( i_type       = 'TXT'
                                            i_subject    = 'Testing'
                                            i_language   = sy-langu
                                            i_importance = '1' ).
    req->set_document( doc ).

*   Add sender and recipient
    sender = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( i_address_string = 'someone@somewhere.com' ).
    req->set_sender( sender ).
    recipient = cl_cam_address_bcs=>create_internet_address( i_address_string = 'anyone@anywhere.com' ).
    req->add_recipient( recipient ).

*   Initiate sending of document
    sent = req->send( ).
    IF sent IS NOT INITIAL.
      COMMIT WORK.
    ENDIF.

  CATCH cx_bcs.
*   Handle exceptions here
ENDTRY.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Recreate dunning notices after completing the dunning...
entry.content:
Dear Nathalie,

For a very large volume the recommended standard approach is to use Manage Output Items app rather than reissuing each notice individually from Create Dunning Notices. The output screen in Create Dunning Notices is suitable for individual corrections within the same dunning run but Manage Output Items app is the central app for processing multiple output items at once. SAP documents that this app supports mass processing, sending/retrying output and duplicating completed output items for reissue

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/1c9d0d58fb999d44e10000000a4450e5.html

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003063524

 

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: The tax code maintained at the freight condition type level is not being copied from the PO to MIRO.
entry.content:
I have maintained two separate tax codes in the Purchase Order using the access sequence. One tax code is maintained at the PO item level (Invoice tab) for input VAT, and another tax code is maintained at the condition type level for freight (ZFR1).
 
However, while posting MIRO with reference to the Purchase Order, the system copies only the PO item‑level tax code (from the Invoice tab) into MIRO. The tax code maintained at the freight condition type level is not being copied to the MIRO document.
 
Please clarify whether this is standard SAP behavior, or if there is any configuration or enhancement through which the tax code maintained at the condition type level can also be determined and transferred to MIRO.

entry.title: 🇺🇦 SAP Cloud ALM тепер українською
entry.content:
Маємо чудову новину: інтерфейс SAP Cloud ALM підтримує українську мову.
Офіційний розділ Availability на SAP Help містить українську в переліку мов: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/availability?q=ukrainian
 

Що таке SAP Cloud ALM?

SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management, керування життєвим циклом застосунків) — це сучасне хмарне рішення для керування життєвим циклом застосунків, що допомагає спростити планування, впровадження та експлуатацію хмарних і гібридних бізнес-рішень

Чому український інтерфейс — це важливо?

  • Швидше навчання та онбординг: україномовні користувачі легше опановують інструмент.
  • Зручність для команд і клієнтів в Україні: документація та інтерфейс доступні мовою роботи.
  • Краща комунікація між бізнесом та ІТ завдяки узгодженій термінології в рідній мові.

Досліджуйте продукт і працюйте комфортно — тепер SAP Cloud ALM говорить українською! 🇺🇦✨

entry.title: Re: Data flow from Custom MDF to talent flags?
entry.content:
Hi!

Business rules can be created to fetch/synchronize data from standard EC objects and store that on custom MDF objects fields. But the vice versa - (Data flow from custom MDF objects to standard EC objects) is not supported.

Custom MDF Objects can't fetch/synchronize with another Custom MDF Objects. This is not supported.

Data flow from EP portlets or Background elements to MDF portlets is not supported.

 

In this KBA you can find all the relevant information:

2562069 - Scenario based business rules for MDF objects, Parameters, Limitations and other information about custom MDF Business Rule configuration scenario - SAP for Me

Let me know if you need any help.

Best Regards,
Tündi
Product Support - People | Platform
SAP Support

entry.title: Influence Tomorrow’s ERP Technology with SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1-2026
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA is transforming the way businesses operate. By delivering intelligent automation, real-time analytics, and seamless integration across processes, it enables organizations to drive efficiency, agility, and innovation at scale. Now, you can help shape the future of enterprise resource planning.

Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is still open, offering SAP customers and partners the unique opportunity to directly influence the evolution of SAP S/4HANA. Collaborate with SAP product teams, share your insights early, and contribute to the development of new features and enhancements.

This is more than just a preview – it’s your chance to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of intelligent ERP solutions.

Registration is open until March 13, 2026. 

Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ERP Cloud Delivery & Excellence 

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • Business Partner and Product Maser in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite

ERP - Cross Topics

  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement

ERP Finance & Risk

  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

ERP Private Cloud

  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • E-Mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

ERP Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: Supplier Dow Payment with Charge Off Difference- SAP...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Here's where you find the relevant information:

Clear Outgoing Payments
Handling of Partial Down Payments
Transfer Posting with Clearing
 
And here's how to proceed:
 
In the Open the Clear Outgoing Payments app, enter the amount you wish to pay in the 'Allocated Amount' field. 
This will post the amount as a partial payment, leaving the original supplier down payment request and the partial payment open until the remaining amount is paid.
 
Enter the remaining open amount in the 'Residual Amount' field to create an open item for the difference.
 
You can enable transfer posting to write off the difference due to a charge-off.
 
After entering all details, you can simulate the resulting journal entry and post it.
 
Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: SAP Fiori Development newsletter issue #39 (March 2026)
entry.content:
As we get into the rhythm of 2026, it seems that AI is addressing more coding use cases than even a few months ago. This will be on full display at UI5con, where AI topics will likely comprise a higher percentage of the sessions than last year. Learn more about UI5con at https://openui5.org/ui5con/faq.html, including how to attend, sponsor, or propose a topic for a session.

If you want to join other developers to discuss various traditional and AI-powered Fiori development-related topics, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable. Register online or email me for an invitation.

Development News

Mastering filtering and search capabilities in SAP Fiori elements for OData V4

The filter bar is the primary component for data filtering in SAP Fiori elements applications. It appears at the top of list report page applications and provides users with an intuitive interface to narrow down large datasets. This article discusses search capability, support for the Clear button, custom filters, and of course, how the filter bar can leverage AI capabilities. Learn more.

Getting past the limitations of using AI for code generation

Dirk Roeckmann shares his perspectives on coding with AI agents based on transformer models. He starts with why he uses AI and then shares some limitations of AI models and how to get around these pitfalls. Read his insights.

UI5 MCP server in The Open Source Way podcast

This episode covers the intricacies of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) within the SAP ecosystem, a pivotal component designed to offer domain expertise to LLMs. Join Tim Back and Florian Vogt as we unpack the role and significance of MCP in the SAP ecosystem in the world of AI. (UI5 MCP server blog post, GitHub)

  • Spotify: UI5 & MCP - Navigating the Model Context Protocol in SAP
  • Apple Podcast: UI5 & MCP - Navigating the Model Context Protocol in SAP

UI5 MCP server extended for UI Integration Cards generation

The UI5 MCP Server now supports the development of UI Integration Cards. It includes three new tools to assist Large Language Models (LLMs) in creating new cards from scratch or editing existing ones, following the best practices for card development. See details.

Configure Your SAP Fiori Elements Apps: Introducing Fine-Tuning in the SAP Fiori Development Portal

Annotations in SAP Fiori elements applications define what data to display, how to structure it, and which features to enable. However, not every UI behavior can or should be controlled through annotations. Some aspects require configuration settings that fine-tune how building blocks render and behave. Learn more.

New geomap control for your SAPUI5 app

A new control in the MDC library has been released. It is displaying interactive maps — visualizing GeoJSON data, integrating vector and raster tiles from various providers, enabling features like clustering and selection, or handling user interaction — sap.ui.mdc.Geomap offers a native UI5 interface to Geomaps. See details.

Events

SAP UX Innovation Day

We are planning more SAP UX Innovation Days in 2026. This name replaces the SAP Fiori Innovation Day brand we used in 2024 and 2025. Watch your inbox for announcements about where and when we will be hosting these events. Most will occur in the fall, both before and after SAP TechEd.

New Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI project): "Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users" Provide feedback on new and planned features in SAPUI5 flexibility - Key user adaptation: Register here.

Back issues from the past year

SAP Fiori development newsletter January 2026 (issue #38)

SAP Fiori development newsletter November 2025 (issue #37)

SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2025 (issue #36)

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2025 (issue #35)

SAP FIori development newsletter May 2025 (Issue #34)

SAP Fiori development newsletter March 2025 (Issue #33)

entry.title: Re: Retrieve custom date value from story filter in SAC?
entry.content:
//Create Global Script function, add DimName as string argument

var AppFileSource = Application.getFileDataSource("Your planning model here"); 
var FilterSelections = AppFileSource.getDimensionFilters(DimName);
var FilterValues = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);
 
for (var i = 0; i < FilterSelections.length; i++) { 
if (FilterSelections[i].type === FilterValueType.Single) { 
var singleFilter = cast(Type.SingleFilterValue, FilterSelections[i]); 
if (!singleFilter.exclude) { 
FilterValues.push(singleFilter.value); } 
} 
else if (FilterSelections[i].type === FilterValueType.Multiple) { 
var multiFilter = cast(Type.MultipleFilterValue, FilterSelections[i]);
if (!multiFilter.exclude) {
FilterValues = FilterValues.concat(multiFilter.values);}
   }
}
 
return FilterValues;
 
 
 
//Call Global Script function for any dimension in your global filters
var CostCenters= Global.GetDimensionFilterValues("CostCenter"); 
var DateValue = Global.GetDimensionFilterValues("Date");     
 
console.log(CostCenters) 
console.log(DateValue)
 
 
Hope that helps @Sorro 
 
Oscar

entry.title: Re: CDS view data delay: API insert in early morning, vis...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Here's what our internal chatbot suggests:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud processes certain tasks in the background for performance reasons. This can include the processing of IDocs, which are scheduled to run every 15 minutes. This might cause a delay in the visibility of data in your custom CDS view.

The system time zone in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is set to UTC and cannot be changed. This might cause discrepancies in the timing of data visibility, especially if your operations are based in a different time zone, such as UTC+8.

To reduce the waiting time, ensure that the background jobs related to your data processing are scheduled optimally. You might need to consult with your SAP administrator to adjust the scheduling if possible.

While the system time zone cannot be changed, ensure that your operations and data queries account for the UTC setting to avoid confusion.

If the issue persists, please create an incident.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Multiple Address Handling in SAP PSA
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question, and I'm very sorry that it hasn't been answered so far.

I've consulted our internal chatbot. Here's the response:

Activating Multiple Address Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud can be beneficial for managing billing across different regional offices or project sites. This feature allows Engagement Managers to select specific bill-to addresses at the billing element level, enhancing flexibility and efficiency.

Key Considerations

  • Irreversible Change: Once activated, this configuration cannot be undone. It changes how the system handles partner data globally, affecting all projects and not just one.
  • Selection of Bill-To Addresses: When creating a Billing Document Request, the system allows you to select from multiple valid addresses maintained in the Business Partner master record. This selection is facilitated by enhanced partner determination procedures and address selection functions in the relevant apps. For more information, see Multiple Address Handling in SD Documents Using SAP Business Partner.

Potential Issues and Insights

  • System Limitations: Some users have reported issues with billing splits due to multiple addresses,  which is a known system limitation. Ensure that the addresses are correctly configured to avoid such issues. For more information, see 3307017 - Sales Documents with multiple addresses are causing Billing Split in S/4HANA Cloud.
  • Preparation and Consolidation: Before activation, it is recommended to consolidate existing master data and enrich it with address-dependent data to ensure smooth transition and operation.

Advice from the Community

  • Thorough Testing: Conduct thorough testing in a sandbox environment before making the change in the production system.
  • Consult Documentation: Review SAP Notes and documentation related to multiple address handling to understand all implications and restrictions.

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Variable Pay Employee History Rule – Custom MDF Object Not Evaluated with Correct Effective Date
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are currently facing an issue in SuccessFactors Variable Pay related to the Employee History rule and the evaluation of a custom MDF object.

In our scenario, we are using a custom MDF object as part of the Employee History eligibility rule. For one employee, a record was created in Manage Data with an effective date of 01/28/2026.

However, when checking the Rule Trace, we observed that the system retrieves the MDF record as of 01/01/2026 (last job info record) instead of the actual effective date of the record. Because of this, the expected value is not found during rule evaluation.

To validate the behavior, we performed the following checks:

  • When we created a record in Job Information with the same effective date (01/28/2026), the Employee History rule evaluated it correctly
  • The same condition used inside Compensation rules works correctly as well.
  • The Employee Central team reviewed the MDF configuration and confirmed that the object setup itself does not appear to be incorrect

Is this behavior expected when using custom MDF objects in Employee History rules?

Are there any recommended configuration approaches to ensure the correct effective-dated record is retrieved?

Any insights or experiences with similar situations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 2: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 and SAP Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
Dear Ganesh,

thank you for your question.

SAP still recommends using business catalogs to define business roles. By combining the best fitting catalogs and switching off any IAM apps that are not required, customers should follow the general principle of only providing least required privileges to any given user.

However, sometimes a business catalog contains a large number of IAM apps while in a certain business role you'd only need one very few of them. Then it might make more sense to ignore the catalog and directly assign those IAM apps to a business role.

Price calculation of business roles is still based on the assigned business catalogs. We're currently working on changing the price calculation from catalog based to being based on the assigned IAM apps. It is planned to switch the price calculation within 2602.

I hope this explains the current situation.

Best regards,

Christian

entry.title: SAP IBP’s HPA 2026 Strategy: Unified Planning and Empowered Adoption
entry.content:
One Unified Planning Area for End-to-End Supply Chain Planning

Harmonised Planning Area (HPA) eliminates the need for separate planning areas (such as the older SAPIBP1 or SAP7F templates) by providing a single, standardised data model for all major IBP modules: Demand, Sales & Operations Planning, Inventory, Response & Supply (order-based planning), etc. Key goals and benefits of the HPA include:

Unified Data Model & Master Data

  •              End-to-End Planning Visibility
  •              Streamlined Integration: Lower TCO & Simplified Maintenance
  •              Improved Performance and New Features

 Why is HPA relevant to customers?

For IBP customers, HPA means faster value realization of a full end-to-end planning process. A single, pre-delivered template (I_SAPIBP2) can cover an integrated business planning process.

Empowering Customers and Partners for HPA Success

To ensure IBP customers and partners can confidently adopt the HPA, SAP has rolled out a comprehensive enablement program as part of the 2025–2026 strategy. This includes:

  •  Early Adopter Care (EAC) Program: Alongside the initial HPA release, SAP launched an HPA Early Adopter Care program (starting around the IBP 2508 release in late 2025) to support customers in implementing HPA and provide them with direct access to SAP product experts
  • Partner Enablement Workshops: SAP recognized that consulting partners play a critical role in scaling HPA to the broader market. In mid/late 2025, SAP organized a series of onsite HPA workshops for partners and SAP consultants in global locations (Walldorf, Dallas, Bangalore, etc.)
  •  Online Help Documentation: SAP has updated its official Help documentation to support HPA.
    • Integrated Business Planning Based on I_SAPIBP2 | SAP Help Portal
  • Field and Sales Enablement: Internally, SAP’s field organizations (consultants, pre-sales, support) have been equipped with HPA knowledge so they can better assist customers.

HPA Adoption Paths and Timeline

The HPA 2026 strategy distinguishes between greenfield, brownfield and blue field adoption scenarios, ensuring a clear path for each.

  •  Greenfield: For new IBP customers or new implementations by existing customers, the strategy is straightforward – start on HPA from day one. Since the 2508 release, all the building blocks for a full end-to-end planning process are available in the harmonized area (I_SAPIBP2), and as of IBP 2508 (mid-2025) SAP even set HPA as the default planning area for new IBP installations (with an option to opt out if necessary). Greenfield projects can directly implement IBP using the latest best-practice content on HPA. This approach is ideal for companies that are new to IBP or those who wish to reimplement to achieve a cleaner, standardized design. By using HPA from the start, greenfield adopters ensure they are aligned with SAP’s roadmap and won’t need a future migration – they’re already on the strategic platform.
  • Brownfield: For existing IBP customers with live systems (whether using the time-series planning area SAPIBP1, order-based planning areas SAP7 and SAP7F, or both in parallel), the strategy to a new I_SAPIBP2 planning area involves a transition plan based on tool-enabled consulting services available from SAP and partners. Initially, customers could experiment with HPA in a sandbox (for example, by copying live data into an HPA test system) while continuing to run their business on the current planning areas. As migration tools mature in 2026, brownfield projects can proceed in earnest. The MOVE2H tool (2605 release) will enable SAP to convert SAP7F order-based planning areas to I_SAPIBP2. Subsequent tool updates will handle SAP7 and SAPIBP1 time-series planning area migration by SAP and partners. Brownfield adoption may involve running HPA in parallel to the previous planning area for some period (to validate results and train users) before cutting over.
  • Bluefield: When merging legacy planning area configuration into I_SAPIBP2, some customers may choose a blue field approach – such as standing up a new productive HPA planning area in parallel to the previous one(s) and gradually phasing over configuration and processes. While more manually intensive, this approach allows planning processes to be redesigned, especially harmonized supply planning. The 2026-2027 SAP IBP strategy supports these flexible approaches by providing the necessary tools and guidance for partial migrations, data syncing, and co-existence of old and new planning areas during transition.

 

In summary, SAP’s migration tooling is designed to facilitate both ‘Brownfield’ and ‘Bluefield’ adoption paths for HPA. Brownfield migrations (upgrading an existing IBP instance to a new I_SAPIBP2 instance) will be possible with MOVE2H and related tools, preserving much of the investment in configuration and historical data. Meanwhile, Bluefield or hybrid approaches are also supported, with data load techniques and side-by-side validation approaches. These tools ensure that whether a customer chooses to migrate or re-implement, the path to HPA is well-supported.

Crucially, there is no forced end-of-life date for the previous planning areas, but the writing is on the wall: new IBP features will increasingly be delivered only for HPA after 2025. For instance, some advanced supply planning capabilities are already exclusive to HPA as of 2026. Therefore, SAP encourages customers to plan their move to HPA to stay current and unlock the full value of IBP. The HPA 2026 strategy provides the means (tools and services) to make that move in a controlled and efficient way.

Your Strategic Call to Action: Embrace HPA for Future-Ready Planning

As an IBP customer, you’re at a pivotal moment. The Harmonized Planning Area (HPA) isn’t just another upgrade—it’s your pathway to unlocking faster value realization, improved performance, and exclusive access to cutting-edge IBP capabilities that will define the future of integrated business planning.

Why This Matters to Your Business

The reality is clear: While there’s no forced end-of-life date for your current planning areas, new IBP features will increasingly be delivered exclusively for HPA after 2025. Advanced supply planning capabilities are already HPA-exclusive as of 2026.

With HPA, you gain:

  •              Complete end-to-end planning visibility across all modules
  •              Lower total cost of ownership with streamlined integration
  •              Access to the latest innovations that won’t be available in legacy planning areas

Your Path Forward: Choose What Works for You

New to IBP or Ready for a Fresh Start?

Go Greenfield – Start with HPA from day one. New IBP installations default to HPA as of the 2508 release. You’ll be aligned with SAP’s strategic roadmap from the beginning.

Running Live IBP Systems?

Take the Brownfield Approach – Use 2025 to experiment with HPA in sandbox environments while migration tools like MOVE2H mature. Plan your controlled transition for 2026-2027 when full tooling support is available.

Want Maximum Control?

Consider Bluefield – Run HPA in parallel to your existing planning areas, allowing you to redesign and harmonize processes at your own pace while maintaining business continuity.

Immediate Steps You Should Take

  1. Assess Your Current State Today
  •              Identify your specific migration requirements and timeline
  1. Get Expert Support
  •              Join the Early Adopter Care Program – Get direct access to SAP product experts starting with the IBP 2508 release
  •              Connect with SAP consultants or certified partners for your HPA roadmap
  1. Start Learning Now
  •              Access updated documentation on the SAP Help Portal
  1. Plan Your Strategy
  •              Align your approach with the 2026-2027 tool availability roadmap
  •              Consider running pilot projects in sandbox environments

Don’t Wait – The Competitive Advantage is Real

Companies moving to HPA now will benefit from:

  •              Enhanced Functional Scope
  •              Simplified maintenance and reduced complexity
  •              Better positioning for future IBP innovations
  •              Improved planning performance across your entire supply chain

Ready to Begin?

Your journey to HPA success starts with a single step. Contact your SAP account team for personalised guidance on your HPA adoption strategy.

The future of integrated business planning is here. Make sure your organisation captures its full value.

entry.title: Event Recap — SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days
entry.content:
🌟 Event Recap  of SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 —How SAP Is Enabling Connected, Customer‑Driven Product Innovation

For three days, experts from engineering, R&D, manufacturing, quality, compliance, IT, and project management came together to explore one of the most pressing questions facing modern product organizations:

How do we build products faster, smarter, and more responsibly in an increasingly complex world?

Each day examined this question through a different lens — discrete industries, process industries, and project & portfolio management — yet a shared storyline ran across all discussions. Attendees repeatedly highlighted challenges around fragmented product data, heavy compliance workloads, disconnected processes, and rising expectations for AI-enabled efficiency.

Across the event, one thing became clear: Integrated, intelligent PLM is no longer optional. It’s becoming the operational backbone of modern product organizations.

🔍 Three Cross‑Event Themes That Defined the Week

1. Product data fragmentation remains the biggest barrier

Whether teams manage CAD files, formulation data, specifications, or project plans, many participants described workflows that still depend on disconnected tools, inconsistent handovers, and manual reconciliation. These issues slow development and create risks long before a product reaches the shop floor.

2. Handover friction is still one of the costliest issues

Across industries, attendees shared how difficult it remains to move product definitions cleanly from engineering → manufacturing, formulation → production, and planning → execution. Every time a document, recipe, product structure or bill of materials changes hands, teams lose time, context, or accuracy.

This was one of the clearest signals from the entire event: handovers matter as much as the work itself.

3. AI is shifting from potential to practice

Perhaps the biggest mindset shift was the acceptance of AI as a practical, embedded partner in everyday workflows. Across the three days, participants responded strongly to examples of AI:

  • identifying risks earlier
  • supporting change impact analysis
  • assisting formulation decisions
  • forecasting project schedules
  • enhancing decision-making with natural-language insights

The enthusiasm showed that organizations are ready for AI that is useful, contextual, and integrated directly into their PLM workflows.

🛠️ What Resonated Across the Three Days

Even though each day focused on a different domain, attendees gravitated toward similar ideas — all centered on simplifying complexity and strengthening the flow of product data.

🔧 Day 1 — Discrete Industries

Conversations centered on the importance of engineering data that flows: CAD, product structures, visualization, governed change management, and supply-chain-ready definitions. Engagement grew whenever the link between engineering and manufacturing became seamless and traceable.

🧪 Day 2 — Process Industries

Participants in process industries highlighted the complexity of connecting formulation, specification management, labeling, and compliance. Reactions showed strong interest in structured property management, automated labeling, collaboration scenarios, and native integration with downstream processes.

📊 Day 3 — Portfolio & Project Management

Project and portfolio leaders focused on predictability: aligning resources, forecasting risks, synchronizing plans with execution, and managing ETO complexity. Interest peaked whenever real-time data and AI provided clarity in situations that are normally opaque.

🌐 Bringing It All Together — A Future Defined by Flexibility

Across all three days of the SAP Solution for PLM Info Days 2026, one insight rose above everything else: there is no single PLM journey. Every organization attending navigates a different mix of tools, processes, partners, and regulatory requirements — and SAP’s strategy recognizes and embraces that diversity.

Discrete manufacturers wrestle with CAD-heavy engineering environments; process industry teams manage formulations, specifications, and compliance; and project-driven organizations balance resources, risks, and constantly shifting priorities. Yet despite these differences, a consistent truth connected every conversation:

Product data only delivers value when it is connected — and each customer needs the freedom to define how that connection happens.

That is why SAP’s solutions for PLM approach centers on choice. Rather than prescribing a rigid architecture, SAP empowers customers through three complementary paths:

  • Direct CAD Integration
     For engineering-led companies that want design files, product structures, and change processes flowing cleanly into the SAP Business Suite.
  • Cloud-Native Product Data Management
     For teams looking for collaborative capabilities on SAP BTP — formulation, specification governance, product structures — fully integrated with SAP’s operational backbone.
  • PLM System Integration
     For organizations invested in external PLM, enabling bi-directional synchronization and smooth orchestration of product data across hybrid landscapes.

What ties these paths together is the SAP Business Suite — the system of record that anchors product truth, unifies supply chain and execution processes, and unlocks operational value from engineering and R&D decisions.

The strongest takeaway from the event was not about any single tool or capability. It was about empowerment. Customers want to modernize on their own terms, evolve their PLM landscape without disruption, and integrate what works today with what they want tomorrow. SAP’s portfolio — from SAP Engineering Control Center and SAP Integrated Product Development on SAP Business Technology Platform to SAP Project & Resource Management, SAP Enterprise Portfolio & Project Management, and PLM System Integration — gives them exactly that freedom.

✨ Final Thoughts — How This Blog Series Fits Together

This overall recap, along with the three deep-dive blogs for discrete industries, process industries, and project & portfolio management, forms a complete picture of SAP’s flexibility-first PLM strategy.

Each blog explores a different entry point into the product lifecycle, but together they show how SAP enables companies to build their own path toward a connected, intelligent, lifecycle-driven product development ecosystem.

Rather than forcing customers into a single definition of PLM, SAP provides a flexible foundation — grounded in the SAP Business Suite, enhanced by cloud innovation, and extended through open integration — so every organization can design a PLM journey that fits their industry, their maturity, and their future.

Continue Your Journey with SAP solutions for PLM

If you missed a session or want to revisit highlights, you can still watch the SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 on‑demand.

We’ll continue deep‑diving into engineering, formulation, compliance, project management, and AI‑enabled product development throughout the year. Make sure to bookmark our upcoming webinars to stay ahead of the latest innovations.

To stay connected with the PLM community of SAP:

  • Follow us on LinkedIn for insights, updates, and customer stories
  • Join the SAP Community and follow the Product Lifecycle Management tag
  • Explore the latest product tour on sap.com/plm to see how our solutions help streamline product data and accelerate innovation

Thank you for joining us at SAP Solutions for PLM Info Days 2026 — we’re excited to continue shaping the future of connected intelligent product development with you.

entry.title: Your Blueprint to Lead the Shift in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
entry.content:
The Utilities industry is moving fast toward cloud-ready architectures, customer-first operations, and intelligent,  AI-enabled processes. If you're an architect, business user , or consultant, you're at the center of this transformation.  

The journey begins with a thorough introduction to SAP S/4HANA Utilities, covering unique system capabilities, end-to-end industry processes, and customer service operations. From there, you will explore the strategic differences between SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA Utilities, learning to evaluate conversion options and apply proven transition methodologies using tools like SAP Readiness Check. 

The curriculum then guides you through essential system configuration, including master data modeling, device management with inventory and procurement processes, meter reading, and integration with other SAP components. Billing and invoicing are addressed in depth, from rate structure configuration to budget billing plans and exception handling. 

For the German energy market, specialized training covers market communication processes, including the cloud-based SAP Market Communication (MaCo) solution, Application Process Engine (APE), and the integration into SAP S/4HANA Utilities with a focus on regulatory compliance and automation. 

Finally, the curriculum prepares you to build future-proof architectures by keeping the SAP S/4HANA core clean through extensibility on SAP Business Technology Platform, and utilizing SAP Fiori for a modern, industry-specific user experience. 

Ready to go hands on? 

Build end-to-end skills with the refreshed course stack:  

  1. S4IUT is your overview and entry point to the SAP S/4HANA for Utilities. The course covers  the business processes , Fiori-based functions, AI-enabled optimization, asset management architecture, and cloud integrations: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/executing-business-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-utilities 
  2. IUT21 focuses on master data and basic functions configuration: https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-master-data-and-basic-functions-in-sap-s-4hana-utilities 
  3. IUT22 deep-dives into device management configuration, from master data and inventory to installations, inspections, and meter reading: https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-device-management-in-sap-s-4hana-utilities 
  4. IUT23 covers billing and invoicing configuration, including rate structures, execution, exceptions, and budget billing: https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-billing-and-invoicing-in-sap-s-4hana-utilities 
  5. If you operate in the German energy market, IUT51 explores market communication (MaCo-Cloud) with the Application Process Engine (APE): https://learning.sap.com/courses/explore-sap-market-communication-for-utilities-in-the-german-energy-market 
  6. Round it out with the updated S4IUD gives you the strategic and technical insights to guide customers from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA with confidence: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/transitioning-to-sap-s-4hana-for-utilities 

Ready to lead your customers’ transition with clarity and impact? Start with S4IUT (or S4IUD), then progress through the updated stack to build execution and configuration expertise.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements list report controller extension types...
entry.content:
Try this:

 

import ControllerExtension from 'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension';
import ExtensionAPI from 'sap/fe/templates/ListReport/ExtensionAPI';
import XMLView from 'sap/ui/core/mvc/XMLView'
/**
 * @namespace com.jk.apps.accountwfts.ext.controller
 * @Controller
 */
export default class GeneralDataList extends ControllerExtension<ExtensionAPI> {
	static overrides = {
		/**
		 * Called when a controller is instantiated and its View controls (if available) are already created.
		 * Can be used to modify the View before it is displayed, to bind event handlers and do other one-time initialization.
		 * @memberOf com.jk.apps.accountwfts.ext.controller.GeneralDataList
		 */
		onInit(this: GeneralDataList) {
			const view = (this.base.getExtensionAPI().getRouting().getView() as XMLView);
		}
	}
}

entry.title: Trying to understand SAP in simple terms? Ever wondered what SAP actually is?
entry.content:
In this episode of SAP Learning Insights, David Chaviano hosts Bob Szymanski, a university professor from Georgia Southern University and a prominent voice in integrating SAP into academic curricula. The conversation is about the fundamental question: What is SAP? David reveals his desire to present SAP in a beginner-friendly manner, and Bob takes this opportunity to share his insights, experiences and easy to understand examples. Bob presents a clear, straightforward definition of SAP, describing it not merely as software, but as an enterprise information system that enables business processes.  He shares sources for getting more knowledge and insights and at the end, he of course provides his final words of wisdom.

Download transcript

Dive deeper

  • Bob Szymanski on LinkedIn
  • SAP
  • Bob's book on Enterprise Systems
  • Student Zone on learning.sap.com
  • SAP University Alliances (SAP Community)
  • Learn how students can certify in SAP for free with SAP learning hub, student edition 
 

Further resources

  • Visit SAP Learning 
  • Visit the SAP Community 
 

Enjoy listening to this new podcast! 

Apple          Spotify

entry.title: Is It Possible to Set a Custom Temp Directory for lcm_cli Migration?
entry.content:
I have performed a clean installation of SAP BusinessObjects 4.3 SP3 on Linux, and I need to migrate content from the current 4.2igration, lcm, pmw SP5 environment.

I initially tried to use the Promotion Management Wizard (PMW), but it does not work correctly. The process starts but then doeigration, lcm, pmws nothing: it neither migrates the content nor generates any logs. This issue seems to be consistent with what is described in the following SAP Community thread:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/migration-issues-sapbo-4-2-to-4-3-pmw-error-and-missing-relationships-with/qaa-p/14254183#M4930229

For this reason, I am performing the migration using lcm_cli.sh, split into three phases, each executed with its own script:

  • users
  • dependencies
  • content

The users and dependencies scripts run successfully. However, I am encountering an issue with the content migration: the available space in the default temporary directory is limited, and I do not have enough capacity in that path (it requires approximately 2.5x the size of the FRS).

Is it possible to specify an alternative temporary directory, either via a modifier/parameter in the script itself or in the command used to launch lcm_cli.sh, so that a custom temporary path can be used?

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud Certificate
entry.content:
Hi @phil_moh 
I hope you meanwhile received the second email.
If not here are some measure that might help to solve the issue

 

1. Check your spam/junk folder
Sometimes the system emails land there.

2. Verify the email used for the SAP Store purchase
Make sure it matches the account you’re checking in SAP Learning.

3. For missing exam access or login emails, the fastest route is to open a support ticket
If you purchased your licenses from the SAP Store, please use this support form for assistance.

Please let me know if you need further assistance.
Kind regards
Margit
